# India–UK CETA Export Guide — Sattva Global Logistics

Canonical page: https://www.sattvaglobal.in/india-uk-ceta
Published: 2026-07-15
Updated: 2026-07-15
Language: English (India)
Audience: Indian manufacturers, exporters, logistics teams and overseas buyers evaluating India-to-UK shipments.

## Direct answer

India–UK CETA creates broad preferential market access for Indian exports to the United Kingdom, but exporters should not assume every shipment automatically qualifies for zero duty. Product-level eligibility depends on the exact HS classification, the applicable tariff concession, the product-specific rule of origin, acceptable origin evidence and all UK import requirements.

## Five checks before relying on preferential duty

1. Confirm the exact HS code and UK tariff treatment.
2. Check the product-specific rule of origin, including treatment of imported inputs.
3. Prepare the accepted Certificate of Origin, declaration or supporting origin records.
4. Verify UK labelling, safety, SPS, traceability, technical and sector-specific requirements.
5. Calculate total landed cost: ocean freight, origin charges, destination charges, inland delivery, insurance, financing and applicable duty.

## Sectors discussed

- Textiles and garments
- Leather and footwear
- Engineering goods
- Marine products
- Processed food
- Chemicals
- Auto components
- Professional services

These sectors may benefit from improved market access, but inclusion does not mean automatic eligibility for every product.

## What Sattva Global Logistics does

Sattva Global Logistics supports India-origin FCL export shipments with:

- Freight rate discovery and carrier comparison
- Routing and Indian port selection
- UK gateway and transit evaluation
- Origin and destination charge visibility
- Landed-cost inputs
- Shipment and documentation coordination
- Booking-to-departure execution follow-up

Sattva does not certify customs classification, rules-of-origin eligibility, legal compliance or final customs treatment. Those points should be verified with the competent customs, certification or professional authority.
